commands.review_summary:
The PR restores the terminal `ci-timings-summary` CI job, adds warmup-barrier timing output to the helper, updates workflow guard coverage, and documents the restored artifact.

PR surface: Tests +118, Docs +2, Config +50, Other +76. Total +246 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user-facing bug reproduction path. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the artifact job, and the PR's live Actions run proves the restored terminal job now runs and uploads the artifact on the PR head.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review or landing for a protected maintainer-labeled CI workflow PR.

target.title: ci: restore timing summary artifact
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore the terminal `ci-timings-summary` CI job.
- Write the current run timing summary to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` and upload it as a `ci-timings-summary` artifact.
- Run the tokened timing helper from the trusted base revision on pull requests, add warmup-barrier metrics to the timing helper, and update `docs/ci.md` to describe the restored artifact.


## Background

The `ci-timings-summary` job was added in `c3853611ee` (`ci: add runner fallback timing telemetry`) and later disappeared in `330ba1fa31` (`refactor: move canvas to plugin surfaces`), via #78558. That removal happened inside a broad Canvas/plugin-surface refactor; I did not find PR body or review discussion indicating a deliberate reason to drop the timing artifact.

Restoring the artifact gives maintainers a durable, compact run summary for wall time, queue time, slowest jobs, failures, and the `pnpm-store-warmup` fanout barrier without repeatedly scraping the full Actions payload. Keeping the same text in `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` also makes the data visible directly from the run page.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/ci-run-timings.test.ts`
- `node scripts/ci-run-timings.mjs 26608423875 --limit 5`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check .github/workflows/ci.yml docs/ci.md test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts scripts/ci-run-timings.mjs test/scripts/ci-run-timings.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: CI now publishes the run timing summary as a durable `ci-timings-summary` artifact again, appends the same summary to the Actions step summary, reports the `pnpm-store-warmup` fanout barrier, and keeps `GH_TOKEN` away from branch-controlled timing code on pull requests.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on branch `codex/restore-ci-timings-artifact`, rebased from current `origin/main` after #87813 merged.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused workflow/timing Vitest coverage, workflow parsing checks, docs checks, formatting check, whitespace check, a live timing-helper smoke against CI run `26608423875`, and autoreview locally.

Evidence after fix: Parsed `.github/workflows/ci.yml` contains a terminal `ci-timings-summary` job with `actions: read`, current CI lane `needs`, `node scripts/ci-run-timings.mjs "$GITHUB_RUN_ID" --limit 25`, `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` output, and an `actions/upload-artifact@v7` upload named `ci-timings-summary`. The checkout step uses `github.event.pull_request.base.sha` for pull request runs and `needs.preflight.outputs.checkout_revision || github.sha` otherwise, and the workflow guard asserts that boundary.

Observed result after fix: Workflow/docs checks passed, the focused Vitest run passed 2 files / 10 tests, the live timing-helper smoke printed the new `pnpm-store-warmup barrier` section, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: A completed live GitHub Actions run of the terminal `ci-timings-summary` job on this PR after the trusted-base fix; CI restarted on head `0f5a90872e8084f535c4792ffad3a0da831da1c4` and is still in progress.
